evals.prompt: Our order service publishes an event whenever an order is placed. Design the event contract. Other teams consume it to kick off fulfillment and analytics.
evals.expected_output: A valid AsyncAPI 3.1.0 document with a channel for the order stream, a send operation, and an OrderPlaced general event whose payload composes the CloudEvents structured-mode envelope (context attributes + business payload under data). Event type named acme.order.order.placed; snake_case payload properties; reference ids instead of PII.
assertions: Output is valid AsyncAPI 3.1.0 YAML (passes npx @asyncapi/cli validate)
Uses channels, operations, components.messages, and components.schemas
Message payload composes a CloudEvents envelope (specversion, id, source, type) with the business payload under data
Message contentType is application/cloudevents+json
Event type name follows org.domain.resource.verb (e.g. acme.order.order.placed) [#213]
Event is declared as the general category [#198][#201]
Payload properties are snake_case and carry reference ids, not PII [#200][#208]
Each design decision cites its Zalando [#NNN] rule

evals.prompt: Design a data change event for the customer resource: emitted on create, update, and delete. Consumers maintain a read model and need to apply changes in order and tolerate duplicates.
evals.expected_output: A valid AsyncAPI 3.1.0 document with a CustomerChanged data change event. Payload carries operation (create/update/delete), the resource state mirroring the REST customer resource, a monotonic sequence_number for ordering, a hash partition key in the envelope, a declared compatibility mode, and a closed schema.
assertions: Output is valid AsyncAPI 3.1.0 YAML
Event is declared as the data-change category [#202]
Payload includes operation with enum create/update/delete and the resource state [#202]
Payload mirrors the corresponding REST resource structure [#205]
An explicit monotonic sequence_number is present for ordering [#242]
A hash partition key is carried in the envelope metadata [#204]
Payload schemas set additionalProperties: false (closed schemas) [#210]
A compatibility mode is declared on the event type [#245]
Schema is semantically versioned, carried via the envelope dataschema URI [#246]

evals.prompt: We are standing up a Kafka consumer that subscribes to the payments.captured event published by the payments team, which already owns an AsyncAPI contract in contracts/asyncapi/. Author the event contract for our feature.
evals.expected_output: No new AsyncAPI contract is authored. The feature consumes an event it does not own, so the skill references the producer's existing contract instead of fabricating one: it sets the spec's Contract header to the producer contract (read-only) and points the plan's tests at it, with no back-pointer added.
assertions: No new contracts/asyncapi/ file is authored (consumer-only feature)
Recognizes the feature consumes, not produces/owns, the event type
References the producer's existing contract rather than fabricating one
Sets the spec Contract header to the producer contract as a read-only reference
Adds no backward spec-pointer extension to the producer's contract
